observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts by creating an Inventory
Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the
physical inventory in three months or less and require the Inventory Committee to
prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the Audit Team at least
10 days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in accordance with
COA Circular 2020-006; and
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a complete and updated file of all
Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) to determine the accountability of the
end-users.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
verify with the Provincial Accountant the discrepancies of P225,481.84 and
P49,872.04 between the recorded balances of the reciprocal accounts reflected in
the books of the Province and adjust the same to present the correct balances of
the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statement.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete physical count of the Municipality’s PPE annually; and
b. Municipal Accountant to exhaust all records and documents to identify the undetermined prior years balances.
We also recommended to consider hiring the necessary staff needed by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the conduct of physical inventory.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has already designated staffs for the inventory count and monitoring of the PPE of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant has verified all available records and undetermined prior years balances.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was still not able to complete the physical count of the Municipality’s PPE due to voluminous transactions in the treasurer’s office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the three parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor is facilitating the titling of the three parcels of land.
observations.reason: The application for the titling of the parcels of land is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the OR/CRs of all motor vehicles of the Municipality to exert efforts to locate the OR/CRs of the aforesaid motor vehicles of the Municipality; and
b. ensure that photocopy of the OR/CR was kept inside the motor vehicles whenever these are being used/operated.
observations.managementAction: All motor vehicles of the Municipality is already kept and filed with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that any unexpended cash advances are refunded at the end of the year in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid possible loss or misuse.
observations.managementAction: The cash advances of the Municipality are liquidated at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO and the Municipal Agriculturist to exert effort to ensure that the projects enumerated above be implemented as soon as possible since the same has already been delayed/unimplemented for more than three years.
observations.managementAction: The fund was returned to the source agency because the project could no longer be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom. Also, instruct the Municipal Engineer to observe timeliness/proper planning so that projects programmed for the year are fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has implemented the three programmed projects totaling P4,704,191.00 in CY 2021.
observations.reason: The remaining unimplemented project pertains to the Construction of Tamesis Multi-Purpose Hall which was put on hold because the Municipality is waiting for the release of fund by the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR). The P5,500,000.00 appropriated amount by the Municipality is intended as LGU counter-part for the said project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to correct the recording of the DRRMF received from the National Government totaling P4,695,302.00 as Trust Liability – DRRMF under the Trust Fund books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 to achieve correct financial recording and reporting.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has corrected the record in the books of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer to see to it that the process of insuring all the insurable properties of the Municipality with the GSIS are undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has insured its motor vehicles. The guidelines pursuant to RA 656 require 1st class Municipalities to insure the properties of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete physical count of the Municipality’s PPE annually; b. Municipal Accountant to exhaust all records and documents to identify the undetermined prior years balances; d. Municipal Human Resource Management and Development Officer (HRMDO) to consider hiring the necessary staff needed by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is still completing the Municipality’s PPE. The Municipal Accountant is still determining the prior year’s balances; Reconciliation to reconcile the PPE balances in their respective ledgers, schedules and year could not take place since the Municipal Treasurer was not able to prepare the RPCPPE for the year. No additional staff was provided to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer stated in his letter dated February 23, 2021 that he still does not have the necessary personnel who will solely focus on the management of inventory and conduct the physical count. Consequently, reconciliation of the balances could not take place.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to locate the OR/CRs of the aforesaid motor vehicles totaling P10,326,500.00; and b. ensure that photocopy of OR/CR was kept inside the motor vehicles whenever these are being used/operated.
observations.managementAction: The three dump truck were already fully depreciated and disposed in CY 2021. Hence, these were already derecognized in the books of the Municipality.
observations.reason: No action was performed due to COVID-19 pandemic restrictions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor oblige the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer to see to it that the process of insuring all the insurable properties of the Municipality with the GSIS are undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has allocated funds for the insurance of insurable properties in CY 2021 budget.
observations.reason: The Municipality has started processing the insurance of insurable properties only in CY 2021 and had not insured all properties in CY 2020 due to budget constraints.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete physical count of the Municipality’s PPE annually; and
b. Municipal Accountant to exhaust all records and documents to identify the undetermined prior years balances.
We also recommended to consider hiring the necessary staff needed by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the conduct of physical inventory.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the three parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to locate the OR/CRs of the aforesaid motor vehicles totaling P10,326,500.00; and
b. ensure that photocopy of OR/CR was kept inside the vehicles whenever these are being used/operated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that any unexpended cash advances are refunded at the end of the year in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid possible loss or misuse.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO and the Municipal Agriculturist to exert effort to ensure that the projects enumerated above be implemented as soon as possible since the same has already been delayed/unimplemented for more than three years.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties and provide the needed storage area and facilitate the disposal activities, in compliance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 379 to 381 of R.A. No. 7160. Furthermore, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit a copy of the IIRUP to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has processed the disposal of the unserviceable properties and submitted a copy of the IIRUP to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the three parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor gave his comments that he has initiated the preparation of supporting documents for the application for titling of the three parcels of land.
observations.reason: The three parcels of land remain untitled in the name of the Municipality and the processing of the title is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO and the Municipal Agriculturist to exert effort to ensure that the Sustainable Livelihood Program and the projects from the DA be implemented as soon as possible since the same has already been delayed/unimplemented.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO is in the process of choosing new beneficiaries for the Sustainable Livelihood Program. The Municipal Agriculturist stated that all unutilized funds under the DA are currently being implemented and the projects are still on-going.
observations.reason: The original project proposal did not push through; hence, the original list of intended beneficiaries became obsolete and had to be changed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the recorded interest expense, and consequently adjust the computation and related depreciation expense.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had made the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the recorded interest expense and recognized the depreciation expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to maximize the utilization of LDRRMF by proper planning and full implementation of the LDRRMIP as mandated by law to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the community.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO focused on COVID-19 response, hence was not able to fully utilize the Mitigation Fund of the Municipality in CY 2020 and is for continuing appropriation in the succeeding year.
observations.reason: The Municipal focused on COVID-19 response, hence was not able to fully utilize the Mitigation Fund of the Municipality in CY 2020 and is for continuing appropriation in the succeeding year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the designated MENRO expedite the submission of the revised 10-Year SWMP to the NSWMC for approval to ensure that the programs and activities for SWM are based on the NSWMF; and provide assistance for the establishment of MRFs in eight barangays through intensified information campaign and education on the necessity of MRF in every barangay/clustered barangays for the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the revised 10-Year SWMP to the NSWMC and has established MRF in all barangays of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete physical count of the Municipality’s
PPE annually;
b. Municipal Accountant to exhaust all records and documents to identify the
undetermined prior years balances;
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the PPE balances
in their respective ledgers, schedules and reports; and
d. Municipal Human Resource Management and Development Officer (HRMDO)
to consider hiring the necessary staff needed by the Municipal Treasurer’s
Office.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties, provide the needed
storage area and facilitate the disposal activities, in compliance with Section 79 of
P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 379 to 381 of R.A. No. 7160. Furthermore, instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to submit a copy of the IIRUP to the Audit Team.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Assessor to facilitate the titling of the three parcels of land to avoid contingent
claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA
Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to locate the OR/CRs of the
aforesaid motor vehicles totaling P10,326,500.00; and
b. ensure that photocopy of OR/CR was kept whenever these are being
used/operated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO to exert effort to
ensure that the Sustainable Livelihood Program be implemented as soon as
possible since the same has already been four years delayed/unimplemented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the recorded interest
expense, and consequently adjust the computation and recording of the assets’
depreciation expense.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to verify the discrepancies noted in their property records, make adjustment in the books or in the property cards and regularly reconcile their records. We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to complete the inventory of PPEs and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of some property records was already made. Submitted the inventory of movable properties as of December 31, 2019.
observations.reason: Undetermined beginning balances of some properties.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer to complete disposal of unserviceable properties and provide the needed storage area to facilitate the disposal, in compliance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 125, Chapter 7 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory and Inspection of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) was prepared.
observations.reason: Lack of storage area and the IIRUP still lacks appraised costs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting/correcting entry to reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.
observations.managementAction: The correcting entry was made per Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 401-2019-06-335 dated June 3, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445, subject to the authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction: Reversion of the account was approved per SB Resolution No. 119, s. 2019 dated June 10, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, file the request for authority to write-off of the dormant receivables to the Audit Team together with the required supporting documents for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has submitted their request for authority to write-off the dormant receivables to the Audit Team together with the required supporting documents for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the Biological Assets for fair presentation of the financial statement and in compliance with IPSAS 27, provided the authority is secured from the SB Resolution and is supported with the necessary documents.
observations.managementAction: The derecognition was already made per JEV No. 101-2019-06-1284 and SB Resolution No. 120, s. 2019.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the five parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Inquired from Community Environment and Natural Resources Office (CENRO) on the status of application for this special patent.
observations.reason: Constraint in retrieving the documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist, to monitor the implementation of the projects to ensure completion within the contract period and avoid further delay; Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to coordinate with the DSWD on the unimplemented livelihood program to determine whether the project will still be implemented or the funds will be returned; and Municipal Treasurer to return the unused balance to the source agencies or National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer already in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist. The MSWDO already inquired from the DSWD. Municipal Treasurer returned the amount to the National Treasury per deposit slip dated July 8, 2019 and Check Nos. 2763043, 2763044 and 2763046, all dated June 4, 2019.
observations.reason: The proposed procurement of materials was not in accordance with R.A. 9184, hence, the procurement was delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Officer to maximize the utilization of LDRRM funds by proper planning and full implementation of the LDRRM Investment Plans as mandated by law to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster risks, including projected climate risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the community.
observations.managementAction: Utilized the amount of P3,190,062.06 or 43.15 percent out of P7,393,048.23 available funds.
observations.reason: Some projects are on-going or still for implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor expedite the preparation and submission of the revised 10-Year SWMP and that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) designate/Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board (MESWMB) to expedite the preparation of the revised 10-Year SWMP and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) for approval and provide assistance for the establishment of MRFs in the barangays.
observations.managementAction: Updated the plan as data becomes available and coordinated with the barangays for the allocation of budget for the establishment of MRFs.
observations.reason: Gathering of data is yet to be completed and is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the seven parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents and existing policy of other government agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and
the MT to verify the discrepancies noted in their property records, make
adjustment in the books or in the property cards and regularly reconcile their
records.

We also recommended to require the MT to complete the inventory taking of PPE
and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to complete the disposal of unserviceable properties and provide the
needed storage area to facilitate the disposal, in compliance with Section 79 of
P.D. No. 1445 and Section 125, Chapter 7 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the adjusting/correcting entry to reconcile the Due from Other Funds and
Due to Other Funds accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund pursuant to
the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445, subject to the authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan (SB) with a condition that appropriation shall be made
available in the event that claim arises in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant,
file the request for authority to write-off of the dormant receivables to the Audit
Team together with the required supporting documents for evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
derecognize the Biological Assetsaccount for fair presentation of the financial
statement and in compliance with PPSAS 27, provided the authority is secured
from the SB Resolution and is supported with the necessary documents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Assessor to facilitate the titling of the five parcels of land to avoid contingent
claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA
Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Reconciliation to reconcile their PPE records. • MT to prepare and maintain complete property cards and updated for various Property Cards per category of PPE.
observations.managementAction: Prepared
observations.reason: Undetermined there are still balances unreconciled. Incomplete property records. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page No. 40)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in retrieving the records of the Biological Assets. If all efforts fail, request from the Sangguniang Bayan for the derecognition of account.
observations.managementAction: Secured authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB).
observations.reason: The SB Resolution is still requesting COA’s approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and MT to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed period in accordance with the above-cited Circular.
observations.managementAction: The cash advances were already liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to confirm with the respective national agencies if the Municipality had outstanding balances in their books that needs to be refunded, if not, revert the amount of P26,140.00 to the NT.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended funds were already returned to the NT.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to prepare and submit the IIRUP and undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The IIRUP, prepared and submitted.
observations.reason: Lack of storage area.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund within 15 days after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund were submitted monthly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of LCPC establish proper coordination with the officers concerned in the implementation and monitoring of LCPC programs, projects and activities for the promotion of the rights and welfare of children in order to enhance its rate of implementation. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MBO to provide separate budget and utilization report for LCPC.
observations.managementAction: The MBO already provided a separate budget and utilization report for LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, through the Municipal SWM Board, secure the approval of its 10-year SWMP from the NSWMC and make provisions in their plan for the closure or eventual phase out of the open/controlled dump sites within the period specified under the framework and pursuant to the provisions under the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality conducted the WACS for SWMP and implemented the safe closure and rehabilitation of the open/controlled dumpsite.
observations.reason: The preparation of 10-Year SWMP is still ongoing. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 10, Page No. 53)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to: • continue sending demand letters to the defaulting beneficiaries to immediately settle their loans; and • designate personnel who will monitor the loans granted and the payment made.
observations.managementAction: A resolution was enacted for the request for write-off through SB No. 139, s. 2018 dated June 25, 2018.
observations.reason: The beneficiaries were nowhere to be found.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
 Municipal Accountant and MT to reconcile their PPE records.
 MT to prepare and maintain complete and updated Property Cards per
category of PPE.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Assessor to facilitate the titling of the seven parcels of land to avoid contingent claims
that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No.
92-386.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to exert efforts in retrieving the records of the Biological Assets. If all
efforts fail, request from the Sangguniang Bayan for the derecognition of account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and MT to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the
prescribed period in accordance with the above-cited Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to confirm
with the respective national agencies if the Municipality had outstanding balances
in their books that needs to be refunded, if not, revert the amount of P26,140.00 to the
NT.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
MT to prepare and submit the IIRUP and to undertake the necessary action for
the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRM Officer
to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund within 15 days
after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of LCPC
establish proper coordination with the officers concerned in the implementation
and monitoring of LCPC programs, projects and activities for the promotion of
the rights and welfare of children in order to enhance its rate of implementation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MBO to
provide separate budget and utilization report for LCPC.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, through the
Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year
SWMP from the NSWMC and make provisions in their plan for the closure or
eventual phase out of the open/controlled dump sites within the period specified
under the framework and pursuant to the provisions under the above-cited
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to:
 continue sending demand letters to the defaulting beneficiaries to immediately
settle their loans; and
 designate personnel who will monitor the loans granted and the payment
made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: Inventory committee to conduct the physical count of PPE of the Municipality and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31, each year pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGU, Volume 1; and MT to prepare and maintain complete and updated Property Cards for PPE, and reconcile their records regularly with the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory was conducted by the MT and submitted the RPCPPE to the Audit Team for evaluation. Reconciliation was made complete and updated by the Municipal Accountant and MT, however, some of the records were not reconciled.
observations.reason: Due to lack of Property Cards in the Treasurer’s Office and accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the seven parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor processed the application for Special Patent with the Department of Environment and Natural Resources. Reiterated under Part II, Observation No. 2 of this report.
observations.reason: The decision of the DENR is still pending and due to constraints from other government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to: prepare the IIRUP complete with the necessary information and data in the columns provided therein for evaluation, and, in case where technical knowledge is needed for the determination of the true condition or actual current value of the property to be inspected, a technical inspector with the required knowledge and training shall inspect the same; and dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the steps and procedures enumerated under Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 to generate additional income out of the proceeds from sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: IIRUP was prepared and submitted by the MT, however, some records from the properties were not available.
observations.reason: Because some of the information and data needed in IIRUP were not available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MT and the Municipal Accountant to remit immediately to the Provincial Government of Quezon and concerned barangays the corresponding amounts due them. Henceforth, require the same to remit the shares from RPT/SET of the corresponding LGUs within five days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 27(d) of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The MT remitted the shares from RPT/SET of LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC and provide programs, projects and activities for the promotion of the rights and welfare of children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality budgeted at least 1% of its IRA to LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to strictly comply with the requirements stated under the above-cited regulation to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.
observations.managementAction: The MT started using the stamp “PAID” to all paid DVs and its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year SWMP from the National Solid Waste Management Commission and make provisions in their plan for the closure or eventual phase out of the open/controlled dump sites within the period specified under the framework and pursuant to the provisions under the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer submitted the Waste Analysis Characterization Study to the National Solid Waste Management Commission. Reiterated under Part II, Observation No. 9 of this report.
observations.reason: The decision of the DENR/NSWMC is still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; instruct the Municipal Engineer/MPDC to submit the list of all on-going PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: Notices of Award were submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipal Engineer submitted the list of all on-going PPA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the offices concerned, establish proper coordination in the implementation and monitoring of the projects under the 20% DF in order to enhance the rate of implementation and ensure that benefits are delivered to the constituents of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: For 2017 the Municipality of Unisan had 88.70% utilization rate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format as provided in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the LDRRMO to submit the said report to the COA Auditor for monitoring and evaluation; Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary entries to adjust the account to ensure fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF except for the period January to May 2017. The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 101-2017-06-1308 to reflect the necessary adjustment.
observations.reason: Due to work overload in her office. Reiterated under Part II, Observation No. 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: allocate the mandatory five per cent (5%) of the total appropriation for Gender and Development to sustain programs and projects wherein women potentials are recognized, leading to equality among men and women, thereby enhancing its capability as partners in the pursuit of building a better community.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality provided the mandatory 5% for GAD Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the seven parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with the afore-cited regulation.
observations.managementAction: Application for Special Patent for the unititled lots were already filed by the Municipal Assessor
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor is still in the process of securing new Deed of Donations for the two parcels of land and application for Special Patent is on process for the remaining parcels.  Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 2.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Disposal Committee to:

fill up completely the columns provided for evaluation, particularly columns under Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and, in case where technical knowledge is needed for the determination of the true condition or actual current value of the property to be inspected,  a technical inspector with the required knowledge and training shall inspect the  same; and

dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the steps and procedures enumerated under Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 448 of the GAAM, Vol. II  to generate additional income out of the proceeds from sale thereof.
observations.reason:
Due to incomplete physical count of the unserviceable properties.  Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 3.



The Disposal Committee is still gathering data for the disposal of the fully depreciated and unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to officially communicate with the former Municipal Mayor, for the submission of documents and/or other proofs for the accounting of the unaccounted buffer stocks.  Failure of the former Municipal Mayor to comply, we recommended to demand for the refund of the unaccounted amount of P89,812.17. Payments thereon should be properly receipted and returned to the municipal coffer.  Further, we recommended strict adherence to Section 88 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 338 of RA No. 7160 on the prohibition against advance payment on government contracts.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer sent a demand letter to the former Municipal Mayor but the latter did not respond.
observations.reason:
The former Municipal Mayor did not reply to the formal written communication made by the current administration;

No documents were turned over to the current administration.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Inventory committee to conduct the physical count of PPE of the Municipality and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31, of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS, Volume 1; and
Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated Property Cards for PPE, and reconcile their records regularly with the Accounting Office.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of the seven parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to:
prepare the IIRUP complete with the necessary information and data in the columns provided therein for evaluation, and, in case where technical knowledge is needed for the determination of the true condition or actual current value of the property to be inspected,  a technical inspector with the required knowledge and training shall inspect the  same; and
dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the steps and procedures enumerated under Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 to generate additional income out of the proceeds from sale thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to remit immediately to the Provincial Government of Quezon and concerned barangays the corresponding amounts due them.  Henceforth, require the same to remit the shares from RPT/SET of the corresponding LGUs within five days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 27(d) of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC and provide programs, projects and activities for the promotion of the rights and welfare of children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under the above-cited regulation to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission and make provisions in their plan for the closure or eventual phase out of the open/controlled dump sites within the period specified under the framework and pursuant to the provisions under the above-cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and
instruct the OIC-Municipal Engineer/MPDC to submit the list of all on-going PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the offices concerned, establish proper coordination in the implementation and monitoring of the projects under the 20% DF in order to enhance the rate of implementation and ensure that benefits are delivered to the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format as provided in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the LDRRMO to submit the said report to the COA Auditor for monitoring and evaluation.
Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary entries to adjust the account to ensure fair presentation of the affected accounts

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
allocate the mandatory five per cent (5%) of the total appropriation for Gender and Development to sustain programs and projects wherein women potentials are recognized, leading to equality among men and women, thereby enhancing their capability as partners in the pursuit of building a better community.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer is still in the process of completing the conduct of physical inventory of all the properties of the Municipality by reconciling the physical inventory against the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment available from the Accounting records.



The Municipal Treasurer provided the information from the documents available in their office.



The ICO-Municipal Treasurer assigned property numbers for those properties acquired in 2015.




The ICO-Municipal Treasurer started gathering the available records and inquired with the municipal employees regarding the matter.




The Municipal Accountant maintains records of PPE  of each office by just encoding in the computer

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:
	
	enforce collection of  the  receivables from NGOs/POs amounting to P280,000.00 and loan amortization from the livelihood beneficiaries totaling P2,254,143.99; and


continue issuing the collection notices to defaulting beneficiaries including the guarantors of the  loans.

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to institute legal action against the defaulting borrowers.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer continue to send demand letters to enforce the collection of the receivables.



The Municipal Treasurer continue to send demand letters to enforce the collection of the receivables.

No action was undertaken by the Municipal Mayor.
observations.reason:
Although collection notices were sent to beneficiaries, some did not have the capacity to pay.  The Audit Team is still preparing the Notice of Disallowance for the Disbursement Vouchers available on file.

The Municipal Mayor is considering the expenses that the Municipality will incur in filing legal action.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to design and implement a real property tax generation program which should include tax mapping, complete and updated taxpayers records and strategies to improve tax collection efficiency especially on delinquent accounts.

We also recommended to require the Municipal Treasurer to apply the remedies as provided under the above cited provisions.
observations.managementAction:
The ICO-Municipal Treasurer together with the help of the Provincial Treasurer offers compromise agreement to the interested delinquent tax payers.



The ICO-Municipal Treasurer exert efforts by applying tax remedies to improve the collections of tax delinquencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the regulation to deposit all collections promptly and intact.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer deposited the amount of P54,979.93.  Breakdown as follows:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the amount of P9,218.76 to the National Treasury in compliance with the above-cited Section.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer deposited the amount of P9,218.76 on July 26, 2016 under the account of Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year, as of January 1, 2015; and

Submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Engineer submitted the list of all on-going government PPA on July 6, 2016 and were reviewed and validated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

submit the lacking documents to the Auditor for further evaluation.  


ensure that payments are supported with complete documentation before processing them.
observations.managementAction:
The lacking documents were submitted to the Audit Team on June 27, 2016.



The Municipal Accountant ensures that payments of DVs are supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up the necessary entries to adjust the account to ensure fair presentation of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared JEV No. 401-2016-07-624 dated July 1, 2016 to adjust the  affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the Notes to Financial Statements includes disclosures showing the a) changes in budgets; b) material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts; and c) reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements which will guide the users/readers in making sound financial decision.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the additional Notes to Financial Statements showing the discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to:

henceforth submit the Municipality’s GAD Plan to the NCRFW for review and to ensure that the planned programs/projects/activities are in accordance with the existing regulations and guidelines

furnish the DBM, NCRFW and Congress the GAD Accomplishment Report and refund the amount of P50,000.00, representing expense for electricity of birthing facility, which is not GAD related.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD Focal Person submitted the GAD Plan to the Provincial Planning and Development as provided in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2016-01 as amended.



The GAD Focal Person furnished the DBM, NCRFW and Congress with the GAD Accomplishment Report.  She also submitted the justification for the incurrence of electricity expense and upon evaluation, the Audit Team was convinced that the said expense can be charged to GAD fund.
